sanskrit:
अभ्यासयोगयुक्तेन चेतसा नान्यगामिना।
परं पुरुषं दिव्यं याति पार्थानुचिन्तयन्॥
transliteration: abhyāsa-yoga-yuktena cetasā nānya-gāminā | paraṃ puruṣaṃ divyaṃ yāti pārthānucintayan ||
literal_meaning: By the mind engaged in the yoga of constant practice, not going elsewhere, one attains the Supreme Divine Person, O Pārtha, by continuously thinking of Him.
contextual_meaning: Consistent, one-pointed mental rehearsal (abhyāsa) directed toward an internalized sacred image (divyaṃ puruṣa) rewires attentional networks, replacing scattered cognition with a stable ‘divine’ attractor that becomes the default at death.

shadow: Doubt in capacity to sustain focus

psychological_state: Flow-state absorption; ego boundaries soften into a single devotional object; death-anxiety replaced by continuity-of-identity with the sacred.
trigger_scenario: A palliative-care patient uses daily guided imagery of a loving light-being; over weeks the terror of dying diminishes and the last EEG shows high-amplitude gamma synchrony.
anchor_text: Hold one divine image steady in mind until it holds you.
